contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 30
- Description: BigModel.cn enabled in OpenCode (Phase 0 dogfood)
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/truesight_autopilot_transcript/pull/1
  
  Details:
  Wired BigModel.cn (GLM-4.6 / 4.5 / 4.5-air) as a selectable provider in the local OpenCode TUI, dogfooding the Phase 0 plan documented in truesight_autopilot_transcript/PROVIDERS.md before any autopilot code changes.
  
  Evidence chain:
  - truesight_autopilot_transcript/pull/1 introduced PROVIDERS.md §2 (BigModel.cn / Zhipu) describing the OpenAI-compatible endpoint at https://open.bigmodel.cn/api/paas/v4 and the GLM model catalog. This work validates that documentation against the live API.
  
  Setup steps performed (no PRs because config files are local-only):
  1. Smoke-tested the contributor-supplied BIGMODEL_CN_API key against /chat/completions for glm-4.6, glm-4.5, glm-4.5-air, glm-4-plus -- all HTTP 200. glm-4.6-flash returned 403 (not in this tier).
  2. Confirmed glm-4.6 is a reasoning model (returns reasoning_content + reasoning_tokens). Confirmed prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens is reported -- relevant to the Phase 3 usage-logging schema in SCHEMA.md.
  3. Updated ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json to add the bigmodel provider block alongside the existing DeepSeek block. Models registered: glm-4.6 (200K, reasoning), glm-4.5 (128K), glm-4.5-air (128K). API key resolves via {env:BIGMODEL_CN_API} interpolation; no secret in the JSON.
  4. Updated ~/.zshrc to export BIGMODEL_CN_API on shell start. Plain 'source autopilot/.env' fails because line 7 has a JSON-blob value for GMAIL_TOKEN_JSON that breaks zsh parsing -- worked around with a grep | cut -d= pipeline so the rest of .env is untouched.
  5. User confirmed working: GLM-4.6 selectable in /models inside the OpenCode TUI.
  
  Production safety: zero changes to the truesight_autopilot service or the running EC2 deployment. Roadmap Phases 1-4 (provider ABC, usage logging, BigModel wired into autopilot itself) are still gated as documented.
  
  Follow-up signal: actually using GLM-4.6 inside OpenCode for the Phase 1 implementation prompt will produce the dogfood data we need before flipping autopilot's LLM_PROVIDER to bigmodel in Phase 4.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 50.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
